ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-01-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Darrel Horsted for Sean Joyce. Karla Biddle in Propria Persona.

Ms. Biddle stated that she did not file anything and that there was nothing she wanted to say. The petition for final distribution was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-27-2013 Continued To Sep-24-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Darrel Horsted for Sean Joyce (Administrator). G. Cat Stokes for Warner Wilson.

Counsel spoke of Karla Biddle not attending mediation, whether representation was made/not made that Karla would sign a waiver, language in the settlement agreement regarding waivers, Karla stating that she is not waiving her claim, transfer of the property and whether some sort of order should be issued to Karla to file certain documents by a date certain. The accounting petition was continued to September 24, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court will send an order continuing the petition stating that any objections are to be filed by September 19, 2013. The Court would also like a declaration by September 19, 2013 proposing how the Court should address the fees.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Santiago. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-06-2013 Continued To Aug-27-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-04-2013 Continued To Aug-06-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Apearances: Darrel Horsted for Sean Joyce (Administrator). Dennis Vann for Warner Wilson. Karla Biddle in Propria Persona.

Mr. Vann stated that he spoke with Mr. Horsted yesterday, that there is a possible way of remedy, that they spoke of mediation and that he is concerned because they only have one Grandchild present. Mr. Vann also stated that he does not know what efforts were made by the Personal Representative to contact the other two Grandchildren (Nicole Wilson and Kim Lavelle Rutherford) and suggested a declaration of due diligence. Mr. Horsted stated that notice was provided and that they spoke with all three Grandchildren in 2009. Ms. Biddle stated that her Father knows where the Grandchildren are, that Nicole is in Sacramento, that Kim is in Folsom, that she did not know about Michael (Brother) until she read the will and that she does not know where he is. Mr. Vann will contact Mr. Wilson to obtain addresses of the other Grandchildren and provide any information to Mr. Horsted. The Parties stepped out of the courtroom to discuss mediation/the matter. Upon returning, Ms. Biddle expressed concern about others receiving preliminary distributions, stated that she did not understand why she could not have a preliminary distribution and agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than August 1, 2013. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by August 1, 2013. The matter was continued to and the Parties are to return on August 6, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on mediation.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-21-2013 Continued To Jun-04-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Karla Biddle in Propria Persona. Darrel Horested for Sean Joyce. Dennis Vann for Warner Wilson.

Mr. Horstead argued that paragraph seven of the will is not mandatory; paragraph five of the will leaves the home to the children, confirmed that notice was given to the children regarding the settlement agreement, and what is left at issue is between Mr. Warner and his own children. Mr. Vann requested a continuance to allow Mr. Stokes to be present to address the objections to the disbursement. Ms. Biddle reported that she has not received any written notices but Mr. Horstead was able to contact her directly and informed her of today's hearing. Furthermore, Ms. Biddle provided counsel with her current residence and telephone number. The Accounting Petition was continued to June 4, 2013, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Horstead stated that Mr. Wilson has waived an accounting and he will file it today.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

Verified Petition For Order Issuing Citation For Warner Wilson To Appear For Examination At Court Hearing And Testify Regarding Property Of The Estate
Off Calendar Apr-18-2012 Continued To Jun-13-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Darrel Horsted for Sean Joyce (Administrator). G. Cat Stokes for Warner Wilson (Objector).

The Court encouraged Counsel to get together to figure out what they need and work it out. Mr. Stokes stated that he is not opposed to a deposition. Mr. Horsted explained that they needed an accounting with respect to the rents collected by Mr. Warner so that his Client can do an accounting. Further argument was presented. The Court will not make a ruling on the petition. The Court directed Mr. Stokes to file a declaration by his Client or do a new accounting. The Verified Petition for Order Issuing Citation For Warner Wilson To Appear For Examination was continued to June 13, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for further status.

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1463 Newcomb Ave., San Francisco, California)
Off Calendar Jul-14-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Darrel Horsted for Sean Joyce (Administrator). G. Cat Stokes for Warner Wilson (50% owner of property).

100% of the property sold for $310,000.00. The Court confirmed sale of 50% ($155,000.00) of the property based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of 50% of the Real Property (subject to review of the order). The Proceeds are to be deposited into a blocked account. Status of filing of the receipt for the blocked account was set for July 6, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Darrell Horsted for Sean Joyce.

Judge Wiss disclosed that she was on a Professional Board with Mr. Horsted, that her Husband from time to time would refer cases to Mr. Horsted primarly in the bankruptcy field and that there was no fee sharing on those cases. The Court informed Mr. Horsted that the sale could not go forward due to notice and posting issues. The matter was continued to June 22, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Darrel Horsted for the Petitioner.

Mr. Horsted appeared for the purpose of extending the special letters in order to have continued authority to proceed as Plaintiff in the pending civil matter. The special letters will expire on January 31, 2009. Mr. Horsted will submit the order. The matter was continued to January 7, 2009.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, LIMITED APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-05-2008 Continued To Dec-10-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Darrell Horsted for and with Charlotte English. Pamela Cooke specially appeared for Mr. Stokes for Warner Wilson.

Mr. Horsted stated that he was concerned about the litigation matter against the Son for execution of the deed transferring the property, that an early settlement was scheduled and that the matter was set for trial in March. In addition, he stated that his primary goal was to continue Mr. Royce's authority as Special Administrator so that Mr. Royce could continue to substitute in as plaintiff for the Decedent in the litigation matter. Ms. Cooke stated that she had no problem with the Special Letters being extended. The matter was continued to December 10, 2008. The Special letters were also extended to December 10, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Weatherly.
